name: AEC Virtual Tally Room
description: The Australian Electoral Commission's official election results platform — real-time counting on election night and historical results for every electorate, booth, and Senate count going back decades.
whyUseIt: The AEC Tally Room is the authoritative source for Australian election data. Campaign strategists use historical booth-level data to identify voting patterns, target swing areas, and model scenarios. On election night, it provides the definitive count.
funnelPosition: Strategic planning and election night operations
outputs: Election results by electorate and booth
marketerWorkflow: Download booth-level results for your target electorate, map voting patterns geographically, identify which booths swung most, overlay with demographic data to understand why
managerExpects: Detailed booth-by-booth analysis showing where votes were won and lost, with recommendations for where to focus resources next time
demoDescription: Download the last two election results for a marginal electorate, calculate the booth-by-booth swing, identify the five booths where the most votes were gained or lost, and hypothesise why based on local factors

vocabulary.definition: The final distribution of preferences between the last two candidates standing in a count
A specific polling place — results are reported at booth level, allowing granular geographic analysis

beginnerMistakes: Comparing results across elections without accounting for redistribution (boundary changes)
Focusing on primary votes instead of two-party preferred when assessing competitiveness
